title: Situate the question, then stake a claim
evidence: Fill-in FRAME ('The choice between ___ and ___ is really one instance of ___ so I hold that ___') to PRODUCE a new situated claim. No provided draft to transform; a frame is still writing.

title: Situate on your own
evidence: Cold write, no frame: 'when water is scarce, should a region protect its water for food or power?' Produce a fresh situated claim from the prompt.

title: Situate on a NEW source
evidence: New source; produce a situated claim on the workforce prompt. No draft supplied.

title: State a claim that names a real tension
evidence: Fill-in FRAME ('___ [position], yet ___ [cost] so ___') to PRODUCE a new claim naming a real tension. Frame = writing.

title: Show genuine complexity on your own
evidence: Cold write, no frame: produce ONE claim on the workforce prompt whose complexity names a real cost. Generate text from prompt.

title: Show genuine complexity on a NEW source
evidence: New source (water); produce ONE tension-naming claim. No draft to revise.

title: Warm up: write the pivot sentence
evidence: Provides a 'Truth to start from' sentence but asks the student to WRITE a NEW 'and yet ...' sentence granting the opposing truth. The given sentence is a starting point, not a draft to transform; student produces new text.

title: Hold the tension on your own
evidence: Cold write: produce ONE body paragraph on the water trade-off that holds the tension. Generate text from prompt.

title: Hold the tension on a NEW source
evidence: New source (workforce); produce ONE body paragraph. No provided draft.

title: Name the type and plan its moves
evidence: Fill-in FRAME + checklist to PRODUCE a short plan naming the writing-task type and its first moves ('name the writing-task type and plan' is explicitly a writing task). No draft to transform.

title: Name the type and plan on your own
evidence: Cold, no frame: write a short plan naming the type from its tell and its first moves. Produce new text.

title: Name the type and plan on a NEW writing task
evidence: New writing task (single passage); write a short plan naming the type and moves. Generate text.

title: Reshape this sentence for emphasis
evidence: Body EMBEDS a complete draft: 'Flat sentence to fix: "Training helps the young, and it does not help older workers, and a budget must choose, and that is hard."' then a fill-in frame to reshape/fix it. Provided draft to transform = revision.

title: Shape voice with syntax on your own
evidence: 'no frame. Take one idea from the source, WRITE it as a flat compound string, then reshape it.' Student generates their own base text then self-revises; NO provided draft embedded. Write-then-self-revise in an editing-construct (T6 SPOT) lesson is genuinely dual-readable, so defaulted to writing.

title: Shape voice with syntax on a NEW source
evidence: New source: 'Take one idea from the water source, WRITE it as a flat "and" string, then reshape it.' Student produces own base text then self-revises; no provided draft. Same write-then-self-revise ambiguity as idx 8.
